revision.text:
[[Image:Yaho Tenmangu.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The main building of Yaho Tenmangū]]'''Yaho Tenmangū''' (谷保天満宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the [[Kunitachi, Tokyo|Kunitachi]] city, [[Tokyo]]. It is along the [[Koshu highway]]. According to the legend, the shrine was constructed by the third son and [[Sugawara no Michizane]] in [[903]] to worship his father. It is the oldest Tenmangū Shrine in [[East Japan]], and is one of three major shrines in Kanto (two others are [[Kameido Tenjin]] and [[Yushima Tenmangū]]).
Original name of the shrine is "Yabo" though the "Yaho" is the popular name.
{{Commonscat|Yaho-temmangū}} {{Stub}}
[[Category: 903 establishments]] [[Category: Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kunitachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Visitor attrations in Japan]]
[[ja:谷保天満宮]]

[[Image:Yaho Tenmangu.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The main building of Yaho Tenmangū]]'''Yaho Tenmangū''' (谷保天満宮) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the [[Kunitachi, Tokyo|Kunitachi]] city, [[Tokyo]]. It is along the [[Koshu highway]]. According to the legend, the shrine was constructed by the third son and [[Sugawara no Michizane]] in [[903]] to worship his father. It is the oldest Tenmangū Shrine in [[East Japan]], and is one of three major shrines in Kanto (two others are [[Kameido Tenjin]] and [[Yushima Tenmangū]]).
Original name of the shrine is "Yabo" though the "Yaho" is the popular name.
{{Commonscat|Yaho-temmangū}} {{Stub}}
[[Category: 903 establishments]] [[Category: Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Western Tokyo]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Japan]]
[[ja:谷保天満宮]]

revision.comment: moved [[Yaho Temmangū]] to [[Yaho Tenman-gū]]: Use hyphen in accordance with convention
link the shrine, not the station.

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=February 2011}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Yaho Tenmangū
谷保天満宮 | image = Yaho Tenmangu.jpg | caption = Honden of Yaho Tenmangū | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | dedication = [[Sugawara no Michizane]] | founded = 903 | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 5209 Yaho, Kunitachi-shi, Tokyo | phone =　 | website = http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/ }} {{Nihongo|'''Yaho Tenman-gū'''|谷保天満宮||}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kunitachi, Tokyo]], Japan. ==History== Located next to the [[Kōshū Kaidō]] highway, Yaho Tenman-gū claims to be the oldest Shinto shrine in the [[Kantō region]] dedicated to Tenjin, the deified [[Sugawara no Michizane]], having been built in 903 AD by Michizane’s third son. ==See also== * [[Kameido Tenjin]] *[[Yushima Tenman-gū]] {{Commons category-inline|Yaho-temmangū}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|40|48.6|N|139|26|37.2|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yaho Tenmangu}} [[Category:903 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Western Tokyo]] [[Category:Kunitachi, Tokyo]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yabo Tenmangū
谷保天満宮 | image = Yaho Tenmangu.jpg | caption = Honden of Yabo Tenmangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|48.6|N|139|26|37.2|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Sugawara no Michizane]] | founded_by = | established = 903 | date_destroyed = | location = 5209 Yaho, Kunitachi-shi, Tokyo | website = {{URL|http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Yabo Tenman-gū'''|谷保天満宮||}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kunitachi, Tokyo]], Japan.{{Cite web |title=谷保天満宮【谷保天神】 - 東京都神社庁 |url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/kunitachi/5719/ |access-date=2023-01-26 |website=www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp |language=ja}} ==History== Located next to the [[Kōshū Kaidō]] highway, Yabo Tenman-gū claims to be the oldest Shinto shrine in the [[Kantō region]] dedicated to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the deified [[Sugawara no Michizane]], having been built in 903 AD by Michizane's third son.{{Cite book |last=三橋健 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5gvJxI03v4UC |title=決定版知れば知るほど面白い！ 神道の本 |date=2011-01-20 |publisher=西東社 |isbn=978-4-7916-1816-3 |pages=179 |language=ja}} ==See also== * [[Yushima Tenman-gū]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/ Yabo Tenman-gū] * {{Commons category-inline|Yaho Temman-gū}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yaho Tenmangu}} [[Category:903 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kunitachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:10th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Yabo Tenmangū]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yabo Tenmangū
谷保天満宮 | image = Yaho Tenmangu.jpg | caption = Honden of Yabo Tenmangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|48.6|N|139|26|37.2|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Sugawara no Michizane]] | founded_by = | established = 903 | date_destroyed = | location = 5209 Yaho, Kunitachi-shi, Tokyo | website = {{URL|http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Yabo Tenman-gū'''|谷保天満宮||}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kunitachi, Tokyo]], Japan.{{Cite web |title=谷保天満宮【谷保天神】 - 東京都神社庁 |url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/kunitachi/5719/ |access-date=2023-01-26 |website=www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp |language=ja}} ==History== Located next to the [[Kōshū Kaidō]] highway, Yabo Tenman-gū claims to be the oldest Shinto shrine in the [[Kantō region]] dedicated to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the deified [[Sugawara no Michizane]], having been built in 903 AD by Michizane's third son.{{Cite book |last=三橋健 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5gvJxI03v4UC |title=決定版知れば知るほど面白い！ 神道の本 |date=2011-01-20 |publisher=西東社 |isbn=978-4-7916-1816-3 |pages=179 |language=ja}} ==See also== * [[Yushima Tenman-gū]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/ Yabo Tenman-gū] * {{Commons category-inline|Yaho Temman-gū}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yaho Tenmangu}} [[Category:903 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kunitachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:10th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia25]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Yabo Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia25]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia25]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Yabo Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia25]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia31]] [[en:Yabo Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia31]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia35]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3571318]] Wikidata name: Yabo Tenman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yabo Tenmangū
谷保天満宮 | image = Yaho Tenmangu.jpg | caption = Honden of Yabo Tenmangū | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|48.6|N|139|26|37.2|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] | founded_by = | established = 903 | date_destroyed = | location = 5209 Yaho, Kunitachi-shi, Tokyo | website = {{URL|http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Yabo Tenman-gū'''|谷保天満宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kunitachi, Tokyo|Kunitachi, Tokyo]], Japan.{{Cite web |title=谷保天満宮【谷保天神】 - 東京都神社庁 |url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/kunitachi/5719/ |access-date=2023-01-26 |website=www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp |language=ja}} ==History== Located next to the [[:en:Kōshū Kaidō|Kōshū Kaidō]] highway, Yabo Tenman-gū claims to be the oldest Shinto shrine in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the deified [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], having been built in 903 AD by Michizane's third son.{{Cite book |last=三橋健 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5gvJxI03v4UC |title=決定版知れば知るほど面白い！ 神道の本 |date=2011-01-20 |publisher=西東社 |isbn=978-4-7916-1816-3 |pages=179 |language=ja}} ==See also== * [[:en:Yushima Tenman-gū|Yushima Tenman-gū]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/ Yabo Tenman-gū] * {{Commons category-inline|Yaho Temman-gū}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]]]]]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:903 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:10th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] }} [[sn:Yabo Tenmangū]] [[en:Yabo Tenmangū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Yaho Temman-gū}} [[fr:Yabo Tenman-gū]] [[ja:谷保天満宮]]

東京都神社庁 |url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/kunitachi/5719/ |access-date=2023-01-26 |website=www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp |language=ja}} ==History== Located next to the [[:en:Kōshū Kaidō|Kōshū Kaidō]] highway, Yabo Tenman-gū claims to be the oldest Shinto shrine in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] dedicated to [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the deified [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], having been built in 903 AD by Michizane's third son.{{Cite book |last=三橋健 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5gvJxI03v4UC |title=決定版知れば知るほど面白い！ 神道の本 |date=2011-01-20 |publisher=西東社 |isbn=978-4-7916-1816-3 |pages=179 |language=ja}} ==See also== * [[:en:Yushima Tenman-gū|Yushima Tenman-gū]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/ Yabo Tenman-gū] * {{Commons category-inline|Yaho Temman-gū}} ]]]]]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}} [[en:Yabo Tenmangū]] {{Commons category|Yaho Temman-gū}} [[fr:Yabo Tenman-gū]] [[ja:谷保天満宮]] {{wikidata link|Q3571318}} [[Category:10th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:903 establishments]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

東京都神社庁 |url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/kunitachi/5719/ |access-date=2023-01-26 |website=www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp |language=ja}} ==History== Located next to the {{ill|Kōshū Kaidō|en|Kōshū Kaidō|WD=Q1156682|lt=Kōshū Kaidō}} highway, Yabo Tenman-gū claims to be the oldest Shinto shrine in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} dedicated to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}, the deified {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}, having been built in 903 AD by Michizane's third son.{{Cite book |last=三橋健 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5gvJxI03v4UC |title=決定版知れば知るほど面白い！ 神道の本 |date=2011-01-20 |publisher=西東社 |isbn=978-4-7916-1816-3 |pages=179 |language=ja}} ==See also== * [[:en:Yushima Tenman-gū|Yushima Tenman-gū]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/ Yabo Tenman-gū] * {{Commons category-inline|Yaho Temman-gū}} ]]]]]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}} [[en:Yabo Tenmangū]] {{Commons category|Yaho Temman-gū}} [[fr:Yabo Tenman-gū]] [[ja:谷保天満宮]] {{wikidata link|Q3571318}} [[Category:10th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:903 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

東京都神社庁 |url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/kunitachi/5719/ |access-date=2023-01-26 |website=www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp |language=ja}} ==History== Located next to the {{ill|Kōshū Kaidō|en|Kōshū Kaidō|WD=Q1156682|lt=Kōshū Kaidō|1=Kōshū Kaidō}} highway, Yabo Tenman-gū claims to be the oldest Shinto shrine in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} dedicated to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}, the deified {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, having been built in 903 AD by Michizane's third son.{{Cite book |last=三橋健 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5gvJxI03v4UC |title=決定版知れば知るほど面白い！ 神道の本 |date=2011-01-20 |publisher=西東社 |isbn=978-4-7916-1816-3 |pages=179 |language=ja}} ==See also== * [[:en:Yushima Tenman-gū|Yushima Tenman-gū]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == * [http://www.yabotenmangu.or.jp/ Yabo Tenman-gū] * {{Commons category-inline|Yaho Temman-gū}} ]]]]]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}} [[en:Yabo Tenmangū]] {{Commons category|Yaho Temman-gū}} [[fr:Yabo Tenman-gū]] [[ja:谷保天満宮]] {{wikidata link|Q3571318}} [[Category:10th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:903 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
